title: Hard Fork to Protocol Version 11 ('van Rossem' Hard Fork)

rationale.summary: Vote: ABSTAIN. For this HardForkInitiation (active), evidence quality was insufficient for a reliable YES or NO decision. Primary blocker: DRep distribution signal was unavailable for this action. Decisive reason: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "The proposal upgrades Cardano mainnet from protocol version 10.0 to protocol version 11.0 without an era transition." and clears the evidence gates. What would enable a directional vote: Provide independent evidence for: The proposal says UPLC case expressions over Bool, Integer, and Data will improve script performance and produce cleaner script logic; Provide independent evidence for: The proposal says security audits have been undertaken for Plutus BLS primitives and execution costs for all Plutus primitives. Residual uncertainty: No DRep distribution available. Confidence context: score 0.00; confidence 50.0%.
rationale.summary_raw: Doctrine-aware reasoning layer nudged the score by -0.020 (clamped to ±0.05): Hard fork doctrine requires high evidence quality, upgrade path clarity, ecosystem readiness, and a concrete risk register. The claims show meaningful technical rationale and some independently verifiable references, but the assessment marks risk review as thin and the document itself relies on asserted testing and audit summaries while acknowledging ratification depends on future stake pool readiness verification and that reversion is only available through disaster recovery.
top_fixes: Provide independent evidence for: The proposal says UPLC case expressions over Bool, Integer, and Data will improve script performance and produce cleaner script logic.
Provide independent evidence for: The proposal says security audits have been undertaken for Plutus BLS primitives and execution costs for all Plutus primitives.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Initiate an intra-era Cardano mainnet hard fork to protocol version 11.0, named the van Rossem hard fork.
Stated amount: not stated in document

Deliverables: Upgrade Cardano mainnet protocol to major version 11 and minor version 0 while remaining in the Conway era., Make new Plutus primitives from CIP-0109, CIP-0132, CIP-0133, CIP-0138, and CIP-0153 available., Make all Plutus built-in functions consistently available across Plutus V1, V2, and V3., Add UPLC case expressions for Bool, Integer, and Data., Enforce VRF key hash uniqueness and other ledger predicate/rule changes., Improve PPView mismatch diagnostics in cardano-node.
Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal upgrades Cardano mainnet from protocol version 10.0 to protocol version 11.0 without an era transition.
Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The hard fork introduces new Plutus primitives defined by CIP-0109, CIP-0132, CIP-0133, CIP-0138, and CIP-0153.
Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal says all Plutus built-ins, including newly introduced ones, will become available across Plutus V1, V2, and V3.
Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposal says UPLC case expressions over Bool, Integer, and Data will improve script performance and produce cleaner script logic.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal says testing reports show no behavioral regressions, complete specification and implementation conformance for new ledger rules, and correct operation of new Plutus primitives and case expressions across Plutus V1, V2, and V3.

Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal links a Cardano Node 11.0.1 performance report and says it shows no regressions for standard value, Plutus, and voting benchmarks, plus acceptable baseline performance for the new Plutus primitives and case expressions.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposal says UPLC case expressions over Bool, Integer, and Data will improve script performance and produce cleaner script logic.

Independent evidence for: The proposal says testing reports show no behavioral regressions, complete specification and implementation conformance for new ledger rules, and correct operation of new Plutus primitives and case expressions across Plutus V1, V2, and V3.
Independent evidence for: The proposal says security audits have been undertaken for Plutus BLS primitives and execution costs for all Plutus primitives.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal upgrades Cardano mainnet from protocol version 10.0 to protocol version 11.0 without an era transition." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposal says UPLC case expressions over Bool, Integer, and Data will improve script performance and produce cleaner script logic." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
